Tests that `Math.atan(undefined)` returns `NaN`.

# Purpose
This code is a test assertion that checks the behavior of the `Math.atan` function when it receives `undefined` as an argument. The `assert` function is used to verify that the result of `Math.atan(undefined)` is `NaN` (Not-a-Number). This test ensures that the `Math.atan` function correctly handles invalid input by returning `NaN`, which is consistent with the expected behavior of mathematical functions in JavaScript when they receive non-numeric input. The code provides narrow functionality, focusing on a specific test case for the `Math.atan` function.
